03-REQ-005.note: Identity kinds distinguishable wherever platform code observes them; no code path handles an ambiguous-kind identity.

03-REQ-006.note: No anonymous/unauthenticated participants in any state-mutating role, on any runtime.

03-REQ-029.note: Action half (no staging/altering for another team) was already carried by the requirement body and #staging-is-team-checked; this change adds the observation half (filtered-view-only cross-team reads; spectators see no private state, stage nothing). Invisibility-filter mechanics stay with live-game-observation.

03-REQ-030.note: Map-entry-only: existing text already states SpacetimeDB holds no notion of individual operators and Convex is sole authority for the operator-to-snake mapping. Staging attribution records (Agent values SpacetimeDB never interprets) are replay-and-audit's story, not an authority record.

03-REQ-038.note: Team identifiers seeded into a game instance denote exactly one persistent Convex team record for the game's lifetime; cross-game collision immunity follows from global-invariants/spacetimedb-instance-isolation. Matrix alt (code-mechanism) declined: falsifiable cross-runtime behaviour, kept as spec.

03-REQ-043.note: Credential/key material only to intended holders over authenticated channels; token-signing private keys never leave Convex (public verification keys only); per-team game credentials travel only via the game invitation to the nominated Server.

03-REQ-065.note: Map-entry-only: the existing requirement already states read access follows Google identity and never the Server visited.

03-REQ-067.note: Accepted-exfiltration residue: a malicious Snek Centaur Server can inject client-side code exfiltrating data its visitors can read — an accepted trust trade-off (visit only Servers you trust), not a falsifiable behaviour. Retired here as the enforcement-model requirement it qualifies; the user-facing trust prose lands in team-server-management's Purpose (authored by that change).

04-REQ-002.note: Game runtime holds/derives/exposes no platform-wide persistent state; instance state is single-game-scoped and dies with the instance (teardown-after-export bracket owned by game-lifecycle).

04-REQ-036.note: Map-entry-only: resolution via the one shared engine build, no parallel/specialised variant, is the existing requirement; the sole-authoritative-executor half is global-invariants/authoritative-turn-resolution.

04-REQ-065.note: No spontaneous outbound transmission during gameplay; game-end notification and finished-record delivery are the sanctioned egress; serving authorized subscribers is contract, not egress.

04-REQ-068.note: No external system consulted during gameplay; all gameplay inputs seeded at initialisation, never refreshed; token validation uses verification keys obtained at startup, not per-connection external calls.

05-REQ-002.note: Convex stores no state whose authoritative home is the game runtime; one-time post-game import of the finished record is the sole exception.

06-REQ-001.note: Centaur subsystem in Convex is the sole persistent home of bot-side per-team and per-game state, excepting game-authoritative state (game instance) and platform-wide records; carried by the requirement body.

06-REQ-002.note: All Centaur state resides in the single Convex deployment, never in any SpacetimeDB instance; the outliving-a-game half is also listed in global-invariants/single-convex-deployment.

06-REQ-003.note: Centaur subsystem holds nothing authoritative for game outcome; game-authoritative state lives solely in the game's instance.

06-REQ-044.note: Operators read/mutate Centaur state directly through the function contract under their own identity, never routed through their team's Server; per-mutation authorization enforcement is identity-and-authorization's story.

06-REQ-045.note: Game instance never reads/writes Centaur state; bot-to-game communication flows only through staged moves and the instance's subscriptions, never through Convex.

06-REQ-046.note: Write direction of the same boundary: no Centaur-subsystem mutation writes game-instance-owned state (dedupe with 06-REQ-045; one boundary, both directions in the requirement body).

07-REQ-061.note: Bot compute for a team consumes only that team's authorized filtered view — never other teams' game state (including views a co-hosting Server legitimately holds) nor the masked portion of its own view. Bounded by global-invariants/server-trust-boundary: binds platform compute implementations, not the operator.

08-REQ-080a.note: No application customisation relied on for any security or correctness invariant; this change broadens the requirement from 'never the Server library' to 'never any client's presentation'.

08-REQ-083a.note: Subscription loss surfaced, never papered over with fabricated/stale state; resubscribe and render fresh on recovery.

08-REQ-084a.note: The application persists no authoritative state of its own across sessions; all operator-visible state re-derived from the owning runtimes each session.

08-REQ-100.note: Server-side invariant rejections surfaced as explicit, user-legible feedback at the point of action; never silently swallowed.

08-REQ-101.note: Invariant-gated affordance enablement derives from server-held state, not client optimism; where not yet derivable, dispatch anyway and surface the authoritative result.

08-REQ-103.note: Views referencing archived teams render from the persisted participating-team snapshot and present archived status explicitly; never broken references. Matrix alt (accounts-and-profiles) declined: binds every historical view across capabilities.

08-REQ-104.note: Every mutating action from any surface dispatched against the owning runtime's server-side contract under identical invariants; no UI bypass; rejection surfacing pinned by global-invariants/client-truthfulness#rejections-reach-the-user. Integrator-facing parity half (05-REQ-050) is platform-integrations' story.
